summary: Runner-level artifact synthesis has been correctly removed. Final video is now treated as a required upstream artifact, and the runner strictly enforces existence without mutation. Reporting remains deterministic and compliant with orchestration-only constraints.
reasons: src/report/ensure-final-video.js no longer invokes ffmpeg and now strictly checks existence of final_video, throwing an error if missing, aligning with 'no artifact mutation' constraint.
run-pipeline.js integrates ensureFinalVideoMp4 as a validation step rather than a synthesis step, preserving orchestration-only behavior.
CLI test explicitly verifies failure when final_video is not produced by stages, confirming correct fail-fast contract enforcement.
report.test.js verifies that ensureFinalVideoMp4 does not modify existing artifacts (mtime unchanged), ensuring no mutation behavior.
run-pipeline.test.js confirms that successful pipelines require stages to explicitly produce final_video, not the runner.
pipeline_report.json generation remains deterministic and includes required fields (stages, timing, contracts snapshot, artifact reference).
Implementation fully aligns with plan constraints: runner orchestrates and validates only, all processing logic remains in stages/subprojects.
Provided code and tests under allowed_paths are sufficient to validate acceptance criteria and architectural compliance.
